skill.name: frontend-guardrails
skill.description: Frontend standards for building, extending, and reviewing UI projects with consistent quality, security, accessibility, and maintainability.
skill.summary: Applies practical frontend guardrails for UI quality, security, accessibility, and maintainability.
skill.author: ieze-king

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk indicators, but review found markdown guidance and checklist text. The confirmed risk is low because commands and third-party tools require human review. No prompt injection, exfiltration, or malware intent was found.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Detections Are Markdown Guidance
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged inline backtick text and command examples. These files contain prose guidance, not Ruby or shell execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced files are Markdown policy text and contain no executable scripts. Tool recommendations require human review before adoption.

low_findings.title: Browser Storage Alerts Are Defensive Recommendations
low_findings.description: Browser storage references warn against storing auth tokens there. The skill recommends httpOnly cookies and does not read or write storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is protective guidance. It discourages localStorage and sessionStorage for tokens rather than implementing storage access.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Alerts Are Token Matches
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are substring matches in Markdown, HTML attributes, and frontend terms. No crypto API or OS probing was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines document ARIA, sanitization, stores, and responsive design. I found no exfiltration, prompt injection, or executable reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: Frontend projects drift when UI, security, accessibility, and state decisions are made case by case. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a practical checklist for consistent frontend work.
actual_capabilities: Detects the frontend stack and selects React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, or generic guidance.
Promotes reusable components, design tokens, responsive layouts, and clean separation of concerns.
Adds security guardrails for secrets, auth boundaries, XSS, token storage, and HTTPS.
Provides accessibility, notification, performance, SEO, and type-safety checklists.
Encourages build, typecheck, lint, and dependency audit verification before completion.
Routes specialized work to mature tools instead of replacing them with prose.
limitations: Does not execute audits or tests by itself.
Does not replace a framework linter, accessibility scanner, or security scanner.
May need scaling down for prototypes, demos, or beginner projects.
Third-party tool recommendations still require human review.

use_cases.title: Scaffold a New Frontend Feature
use_cases.description: Apply consistent component structure, responsive behavior, loading states, and design-token usage from the first implementation.

use_cases.title: Review Existing UI Work
use_cases.description: Check a completed interface for duplicated logic, unsafe browser patterns, accessibility gaps, and missing verification steps.

use_cases.title: Guide AI Coding Sessions
use_cases.description: Keep assistant output aligned with project conventions while avoiding over-engineering and unrelated code changes.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Basic Frontend Standards
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for a small UI change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-guardrails while making this UI change. Match the existing stack, keep the component simple, and check responsiveness before finishing.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Feature With Security Boundaries
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task that touches user data or authentication.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-guardrails for this feature. Keep secrets and authorization enforcement out of the browser, and route data access through the project API layer.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Frontend Pull Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Review task for already written code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this frontend change with frontend-guardrails. Focus on reuse, design tokens, accessibility, state placement, security basics, and verification gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Align a Multi-Framework Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced task involving architecture and conventions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-guardrails to plan this migration. Detect current conventions, preserve valid project choices, identify risky trade-offs, and propose scoped verification steps.

output_examples.input: Add a settings form to an existing React app.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan that reuses existing form components, keeps validation inline, uses design tokens, and confirms build and typecheck steps.

output_examples.input: Review a Vue page that stores a session token in browser storage.
output_examples.output: A risk note explaining the token exposure problem, a safer cookie-based approach, and a request for confirmation before changing the auth flow.
A recommendation to centralize notifications, choose inline, toast, banner, or modal by severity, and add accessible live-region behavior.

best_practices: Read the matching framework reference before applying framework-specific advice.
Scale rigor to the project size and avoid adding infrastructure without clear need.
Verify build, typecheck, responsiveness, and security basics before marking work complete.
anti_patterns: Using the skill as permission to rewrite unrelated architecture.
Treating frontend route guards or hidden buttons as real authorization.
Adding scanners, plugins, or dependencies without user approval and project fit.

faq.question: Does this skill generate UI designs?
faq.answer: It guides frontend quality and implementation standards. It can work beside a dedicated design or scaffolding skill.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it cover?
faq.answer: It includes guidance for React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, SvelteKit, Nuxt, Next.js, and generic frontend projects.

faq.question: Can it replace security testing?
faq.answer: No. It gives frontend security guardrails, but real security review should use dedicated scanners and human review.

faq.question: Will it force a new stack?
faq.answer: No. The instructions say to detect the existing stack and follow project conventions first.

faq.question: Is it useful for small projects?
faq.answer: Yes, but its own guidance says to scale rigor down and avoid heavyweight infrastructure when it does not fit.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It recommends verification steps and tool checks, but execution depends on the assistant environment and user approval rules.
